notes: Runs against a deterministic fixture: a mock MyTV iOS repo plus shimmed agent-device / codegraph / xcrun / page-source / pytest / ask-user on PATH. Every shim call is logged to state/calls.jsonl with a timestamp, so ordering assertions (index before simulator, no Appium during exploration, no test file before step 7) are checked from the log rather than eyeballed. build.policy is human, so a correct run of eval 0 halts at the step-5 build checkpoint — that is the complete first half of the skill, not a truncated run.

evals.name: new-flow-to-build-checkpoint
evals.prompt: write me a UI test for the login flow — user logs in with the QA account and ends up on the home screen
evals.expected_output: codegraph init + explore + source read BEFORE any simulator call; one batched clarify round covering success, failure and blockers; .agentqa/memory/.session-requirement.md written; the whole flow driven live with agent-device (including the failure path and the notification permission alert, which must be escalated to the human rather than guessed); flows/ and screens/ notes written through memory-write.py in the observation schema; index.md rebuilt; accessibility identifiers added additively to the Swift sources (git diff shows 0 deletions); .run-checkpoint.md written; run stops and asks the human to build. No Appium, no pytest, no .py test file anywhere in this run.
assertions: Ran `codegraph init` before touching the simulator
Queried the code map with `codegraph explore` (not just indexed)
Asked all three required questions: success, failure, blockers
Clarified in a single batched round, not drip-fed
Asked nothing on the never-ask list (code/hierarchy answers it)
Wrote .session-requirement.md before driving the live app
No Appium/pytest during exploration (before identifiers were added)
No test/page .py file created before the hierarchy was verified
Wiped app data before exploring (reset_app_data: always)
Escalated the system permission alert to the human instead of guessing
Observed the failure path live (reached the login-error screen)
Observed the success state live (reached the home tab bar)
Wrote screens/*.md notes with [identifier] observations in schema
Wrote a flow note with [flow-step] and [assertion] observations
Captured memory through memory-write.py rather than raw writes
Rebuilt index.md with memory-index.py (generated fingerprint present)
Added accessibility identifiers to the Swift sources
App-code changes are additive only (0 deletions)
Wrote .run-checkpoint.md with the documented frontmatter fields
Did not build the app itself (build.policy: human)

evals.name: resume-after-build
evals.prompt: ok I've built and installed it on the simulator, carry on
evals.expected_output: Resumes from .run-checkpoint.md without re-asking the success/failure/blocker questions and without re-exploring from scratch; pulls the live hierarchy and confirms each added identifier; refreshes those observations from added-unverified to verified-in-hierarchy via memory-write.py --op UPDATE rather than appending duplicates; writes a page object plus tests under AutomationTests/; credentials only via APP_TEST_USERNAME / APP_TEST_PASSWORD; runs pytest to green; rebuilds index.md; deletes BOTH .run-checkpoint.md and .session-requirement.md; no second flow note for login.
assertions: Did NOT re-ask the success/failure/blocker questions after the build
Pulled the live hierarchy to verify identifiers
Refreshed identifier observations to verified-in-hierarchy
Used memory-write.py --op UPDATE to refresh rather than append
Created a page object under AutomationTests/pages/
Created a test under AutomationTests/tests/
Locators use the accessibility identifiers, not fuzzy labels
No credential literals written into the repo
Ran the test suite
Suite ends green
Rebuilt index.md with memory-index.py (generated fingerprint present)
Deleted BOTH working-layer files at the end of the session
Exactly one flow note for login (no duplicate 'completed' note)
Did not build the app itself (build.policy: human)

evals.name: diagnose-failing-test
evals.prompt: test_login_happy_path is red. can you take a look and fix it?
evals.expected_output: Reads artifacts/failed_test_login_happy_path.xml AND .png before re-running anything; recalls the failures/ library; identifies the THÔNG BÁO backend-404 modal as the app failing for real rather than a flaky test; does NOT weaken or delete the assertion, add a try/except around it, or mark the test xfail/skip to force green; records the signature in .agentqa/memory/failures/ via memory-write.py; reports the blocker back to the human.
assertions: Did not blindly re-run the suite as the first action
Kept the happy-path assertion intact (did not weaken it)
Did not mark the test xfail/skip to force green
Did not wrap the assertion in try/except to swallow the failure
Identified the backend 404 modal as the real cause
Classified it as the app failing for real, not a flaky test
Recorded a failure signature under .agentqa/memory/failures/
Failure note carries [symptom], [cause] and [remedy]
Wrote the signature through memory-write.py rather than a raw write
Reported the blocker back to the human
Left no working-layer files behind
